data.body:
# Session summary (2026-06-29)

Worktree: .claude/worktrees/assignee-unify-and-default. Two PRs merged to main: #89, #91.

## 1. Closed two stale in-progress tickets
- busy-taboo-tag (3d3d, story: plan-review threshold calibration) and dear-name-ladle (59bc, bug: verify-step budget misclassification) were in_progress but unassigned. Closed both; completion-verifier PASS signed on each. (Bug close requires a `Fixed:`/`Escalated to user:` reason.)

## 2. Investigation: why in-progress tickets showed empty assignee
CONFIRMED HYPOTHESIS: Jira sync reverts a rebar assignee when it is not a uniquely-resolvable Jira user. Evidence:
- Event log for 3d3d: claim wrote EDIT {assignee:"joe"}; ~143s later a reconciler-authored EDIT wrote {assignee:""} — the clobber.
- Live API: validate_assignee_exists("joe") REJECTED ("3 Joes, ambiguous"); "joeoakhart@navapbc.com" RESOLVED to accountId 712020:6471376f-...
- Live reproduction on a throwaway ticket (REB-530): local "joe" + Jira unassigned -> one reconcile pass -> reconciler EDIT cleared local to "". Cleaned up (deleted REB-530, archived the test ticket).
- Mechanism: outbound treats unmappable assignee as unassigned; inbound differ then sees Jira-unassigned != local and writes assignee="" back (apply_inbound.py). Fix = use a Jira-resolvable identity (email/accountId), not a bare handle.
- WRINKLE: CREATE path diverged from UPDATE — CREATE passed the raw handle to ACLI/Jira (fuzzy-matched/defaulted to joeoakhart), UPDATE strictly resolved (unmappable -> unassigned). Filed as bug 544e.

## 3. Incidental corruption discovered + recovered
During the live reconcile experiment, a concurrent reconciler push-retry left .tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/prev_snapshot.json with stash conflict markers + an unmerged (UU) index — wedging reconcile (fail-closed) AND all store writes ("git commit failed while holding lock"). HEAD's committed copy was clean, so recovered locally with `git checkout HEAD -- .bridge_state/prev_snapshot.json` (no shared state touched). Filed as bug 6818.

## 4. Tickets created
- e6a0 (grumpy-site-beard, bug): completion verdict signature clobbers the plan-review attestation (single LWW signature slot at reducer/_processors.py:376 process_signature). Should be additive. OPEN.
- 6818 (filmy-basin-chasm, bug): push-retry stash-pop corruption. FIXED+CLOSED (PR #91).
- 544e (balm-feast-mica, bug): unify CREATE/UPDATE assignee resolution. FIXED+CLOSED (PR #89).
- c36c (stun-wraith-conch, story): add ticket.default_assignee + claim fallback. DONE+CLOSED (PR #89).
- 38ec (crust-fetch-stump, epic, PLACEHOLDER, OPEN): auto-track a claim<->session relationship from env vars. Grounded in finding that rebar stores NO session id distinct from assignee/author/env_id (env_id is the tracker init-environment id, written once at init; session_log pointer .rebar/current_session_log is local + not linked to claims).

## 5. PR #89 (merged) — assignee unification + default_assignee
- 544e: CREATE now resolves via the same validate_assignee_exists as UPDATE (project scope); resolvable->accountId, unmappable(AssigneeNotFoundError)->omit (unassigned), transient errors propagate. acli.py create_issue.
- c36c: new ticket.default_assignee config key (file + env REBAR_DEFAULT_ASSIGNEE in _CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES; env>file). claim uses it when --assignee omitted; resolved at TOP of claim_compute (before parent-cascade so parents inherit it); explicit "" clears (None sentinel from _parse_assignee distinguishes omitted vs explicit-empty); claim_cli uses claim_compute's return for output. Library claim threads None through. Configured joeoakhart@navapbc.com as the project default in rebar.toml. docs/config.md notes it applies at CLAIM not create.
- Plan-review PASSED on c36c (9 advisories, all folded in: cascade-top placement, None sentinel, cli output, co-land w/ 544e, canonical env name).
- RED->GREEN discipline. One CI-only failure (test_bridge_acli_field_coverage::test_acli_create_sends_assignee hard-coded raw assignee) reproduced via full suite + fixed (mock the search, assert resolved accountId).

## 6. joe-debug on 6818 -> PR #91 (merged)
- Root cause PROVEN: push.py non-FF retry stash->merge->pop ran `git stash pop` unconditionally/unchecked. On clean-merge2 (rc 0) a stashed regenerable .bridge_state/* edit conflicts with merged upstream -> pop applies-with-conflict (stash-style markers, UU, kept stash); merge --abort skipped (merge2 rc 0). Deterministic local-git reproduction drove the real push_tickets_branch and matched the report exactly. Rivals discriminated: H2 (merge conflict) uses HEAD/origin labels + self-heals; H4 abort can't help on clean-merge2; H3 (bridge-state tracked) is the latent condition.
- Fix (two halves): push.py _resolve_conflicted_pop detects conflicted pop, restores conflicted paths to merged HEAD via `git rm --cached` THEN `git checkout HEAD --` (bare checkout alone does NOT reliably clear a UU — verified), drops the stash. event_append.py _recover_from_unmerged self-heals a pre-existing regenerable .bridge_state/* UU on commit (retry once) or raises an actionable error for real ticket-data conflicts.
- Held-out RED tests (test_push_retry_stash_pop.py, test_store_write_unmerged_recovery.py), mutation-checked. Full suite 3756 passed. Closed with completion-verifier PASS; the claim dogfooded default_assignee (auto-assigned joeoakhart@navapbc.com).

## Env / workflow notes
- Pre-commit hook (lint+typecheck) ONLY works with the venv activated; the global toolchain flags a false-positive F811 in passes.py and lacks mypy. Always `source .venv/bin/activate` before `git commit`.
- `make install` does not create the venv; create it first (python3 -m venv .venv) then install -e '.[dev]' (dev pulls [agents]).
- rebar in a new worktree auto-symlinks .tickets-tracker to the main repo store, so ticket ops + completion verifier (reads worktree code) work from the worktree.

## Still open / follow-ups
- e6a0 (additive signatures) — open.
- 38ec (claim<->session env tracking) — placeholder epic, needs decomposition before implementation.
